title: Images must not overflow their container on narrow viewports
summary: AI-generated layouts include images without max-width:100%, causing horizontal overflow on mobile viewports.
instruction: Every image, video, and embedded media element must have max-width:100% and height:auto to prevent overflow.
rationale: Fixed-width images exceeding viewport width cause horizontal scrolling, one of the most common responsive failures. The fix is a single CSS rule.

violations: An with width=1200 height=600 and no max-width:100% CSS, causing mobile overflow.
A marketing section with a fixed 800px wide illustration overflowing the 375px viewport.
controls: An with max-width:100%; height:auto scaling down on mobile.
A picture element with responsive srcset and max-width:100% fallback.
borderline: Background images via CSS background-image that need background-size:cover instead of max-width.

exceptions.description: Images inside canvas/SVG with programmatic rendering, print stylesheets with fixed dimensions, and thumbnail lists where the container controls size.

detector.signalDescription: Find img, video, iframe, embed elements without max-width:100% or width:100% in their CSS.
limitations: External stylesheet CSS may be unreachable by static analysis.
Some frameworks apply responsive styles through utility classes not matching max-width:100%.

scenarios: Images inside width:100% containers where the parent constrains them.
mitigations: Check for both max-width:100% and width:100% (which implies max-width in a constrained parent).

fixturePlan.description: A responsive page with and no max-width CSS.
Same page with img { max-width: 100%; height: auto; }.
A page with inside a parent with max-width:1200px.
